data.reasoningText:
The project is missing `<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>`. Let me add that so it compiles, or I can just add a `using System;` to the source file. Actually, looking at the csproj, it doesn't have `ImplicitUsings` set. Let me just add the using directive.

Wait, but the user's question is about analyzing the test coverage, not about fixing the build. Let me focus on the analysis. The build issue is just because of the .NET 10 preview SDK missing implicit usings.

Let me try to fix the build quickly and run the tests to verify my analysis.

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Fix MSTest 4.x API and run tests

data.reasoningText: MSTest 4.x uses `Assert.ThrowsExactly` instead of `Assert.ThrowsException`. Let me fix that in the test and rerun. Actually wait - my task is analysis, not fixing. But I need to run the tests to verify my analysis. Let me just adapt the test to compile.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-1d1024cef53d461ba97eee4b38fc3477 && sed -i 's/Assert.ThrowsException<ArgumentNullException>/Assert.ThrowsExactly<ArgumentNullException>/' AccessControl.Tests/AccessCheckerTests.cs && dotnet test AccessControl.Tests/AccessControl.Tests.csproj 2>&1 | tail -15
arguments.description: Fix MSTest 4.x API and run tests

data.content: All 9 tests pass. Now let me verify specific mutations that should be caught but aren't. I'll test several dangerous logic changes in parallel.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-1d1024cef53d461ba97eee4b38fc3477 && cp AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.bak

# Mutation 1: Editor on system resources gets CanWrite=true (should be false)
cat > /tmp/mutation_test.sh << 'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
set -e
cd /tmp/sv-1d1024cef53d461ba97eee4b38fc3477

run_mutation() {
    local desc="$1"
    cp AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.bak AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs
    shift
    "$@"
    result=$(dotnet test AccessControl.Tests/AccessControl.Tests.csproj --no-restore 2>&1 | tail -3)
    if echo "$result" | grep -q "Passed!"; then
        echo "⚠️  UNDETECTED: $desc"
    else
        echo "✅ CAUGHT: $desc"
    fi
    cp AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.bak AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs
}

# Mutation 1: Editor on system resource gets full write access
run_mutation "Editor+system: CanWrite=false → true" \
    sed -i 's/Role.Editor => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false }/Role.Editor => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = true }/' AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs

# Mutation 2: Guest gets read access (default branch gives CanRead=true)
run_mutation "Guest gets CanRead=true (default branch)" \
    sed -i 's/_ => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = false, CanWrite = false }/ _ => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false }/' AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs

# Mutation 3: User on system resource gets read access (falls through to default)
run_mutation "User+system gets read (remove User restriction)" \
    sed -i 's/Role.User when !isSystem => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false }/Role.User => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false }/' AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs

# Mutation 4: User gets write access on normal resources
run_mutation "User gets CanWrite=true on normal resources" \
    sed -i 's/Role.User when !isSystem => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false }/Role.User when !isSystem => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = true }/' AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs

# Mutation 5: Flip CanPerform ternary (write check returns read, read check returns write)
run_mutation "CanPerform: swap CanWrite/CanRead in ternary" \
    sed -i 's/return writeAccess ? perm.CanWrite : perm.CanRead;/return writeAccess ? perm.CanRead : perm.CanWrite;/' AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs

# Mutation 6: Remove null/empty token guard in ElevateRole
run_mutation "ElevateRole: remove null/empty token check" \
    sed -i '/string.IsNullOrEmpty(token)/,/return currentRole;/{/string.IsNullOrEmpty/d;/return currentRole;/{1d}}' AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs

# Mutation 7: Guest can elevate to Editor with ELEVATE-EDITOR token (change >= User to >= Guest)
run_mutation "Guest can elevate to Editor (lower role threshold)" \
    sed -i 's/token == "ELEVATE-EDITOR" && currentRole >= Role.User/token == "ELEVATE-EDITOR" \&\& currentRole >= Role.Guest/' AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs

# Mutation 8: User can elevate to Admin (change >= Editor to >= User)
run_mutation "User can elevate to Admin (lower role threshold)" \
    sed -i 's/token == "ELEVATE-ADMIN" && currentRole >= Role.Editor/token == "ELEVATE-ADMIN" \&\& currentRole >= Role.User/' AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs

SCRIPT
chmod +x /tmp/mutation_test.sh

# Build once first
dotnet build AccessControl.Tests/AccessControl.Tests.csproj -q 2>&1 | tail -3
bash /tmp/mutation_test.sh
arguments.description: Run mutation analysis on 8 security-critical changes
